moduleSelector.heading2: Practice with AI patients and real-time feedback.
moduleSelector.description: Select a training module to begin your professional medical simulation session.
moduleSelector.completeToSee: Complete a training module to see your history here.

interview.title: Clinical Interview
interview.description: Practice patient history taking, diagnostic reasoning, and bedside manner with AI-driven patients.

cpr.title: CPR Training
cpr.description: Simulate emergency resuscitation procedures, compression rhythm, and AED operation protocols.

aed.title: AED Training
aed.description: Learn to operate an automated external defibrillator: pad placement, rhythm analysis, shock delivery, and integration with CPR cycles.

firstAid.title: First Aid Training
firstAid.description: Practice wound assessment, bleeding control, bandaging techniques, choking response, and recovery position with step-by-step AI guidance.

trauma.title: Trauma Response Training
trauma.description: Simulate multi-casualty triage, primary survey (ABCDE), hemorrhage control, and spinal immobilization under time-critical scenarios.

settings.label: Settings
settings.title: AI Settings
settings.description: Adjust conversation and voice behavior. Changes save automatically in this browser.
settings.dialogueDesc: Patient responses, interview scoring, and session feedback.
settings.voiceDesc: Spoken patient output and voice playback used during training.
settings.autoSaved: Changes are saved automatically for this browser.

interview.chooseLevelDesc: Choose the level of ambiguity you want to practice.
interview.foundationalDesc: Classic symptoms with a clear history path
interview.mixedDesc: Less obvious clues that need wider coverage
interview.redFlagDesc: Comorbidities and risk signals under pressure
interview.roleDesc: You are a medical student conducting a clinical interview.
interview.patientSettingDesc: {{name}}, {{age}}y {{gender}}. The patient will describe their chief complaint when the session begins.
interview.scoringDesc: You will be scored on history coverage, reasoning, diagnosis, communication, efficiency, and safety.
interview.goodSession: What a Good Session Looks Like
interview.goodSessionDesc: Start broad, cover key history, then narrow with empathy.
interview.suggestedOpeningDesc: Start with an open question, then expand into onset, triggers, and associated symptoms.
interview.starterPrompt1: Can you tell me more about when the breathlessness started?
interview.starterPrompt2: What seems to make it worse or bring it on?
interview.starterPrompt3: Have you noticed chest pain, cough, or swollen ankles with it?
interview.voiceNotSupported: Speech Recognition not supported in this browser
interview.exploreDesc: Good. Now explore onset, duration, and triggers.
interview.digDeeperDesc: Ask about associated symptoms and severity.
interview.digTip1: Any other symptoms alongside the main complaint?
interview.digTip2: How severe is it on a scale of 1-10?
interview.tryDifferentDesc: Consider asking about past medical history or medications.
interview.tryTip1: Do you have any other health conditions?
interview.tryTip2: Are you currently taking any medications?
interview.tryTip3: Any family history of similar problems?
interview.strongSession: Strong session. Tighten the gaps below.
interview.goodStart: Good start. Repeat this case once more.
interview.repeatFramework: Repeat the core interview framework before retrying.
interview.analyzingDesc: Evaluating clinical reasoning and communication efficiency...
interview.exitWarning: You are in the middle of a session. Exit anyway?

cpr.roleDesc: Respond to an adult collapse and maintain safe, steady CPR.
cpr.scoringDesc: You will be scored on rhythm, form, recoil, consistency, and CPR sequence.
cpr.keyPoint1: Call 911 first (or have someone else call)
cpr.keyPoint2: Push hard and fast in the center of the chest
cpr.sceneSafetyInst: Look around for hazards. Is the scene safe to approach?
cpr.sceneSafetyDetail: Check for traffic, fire, electrical hazards, or other dangers before approaching the victim.
cpr.checkResponseInst: Tap the victim on the shoulder and shout "Are you okay?"
cpr.checkResponseDetail: Tap firmly on both shoulders. Shout clearly. Wait a few seconds for any response.
cpr.callHelpInst: Call 911 or direct a bystander to call. Request an AED.
cpr.callHelpDetail: If alone, put phone on speaker. Tell the dispatcher your location and that someone is unresponsive.
cpr.checkBreathingInst: Look, listen, and feel for breathing (max 10 seconds).
cpr.checkBreathingDetail: Look for chest rise, listen for breath sounds, feel for air on your cheek. Gasping is NOT normal breathing.
cpr.rescueBreathsInst: Tilt the head, lift the chin, and deliver rescue breaths.
cpr.rescueBreathsDetail: Pinch the nose, seal your mouth over theirs, and give 1 breath over 1 second. Watch for chest rise.
cpr.switchInst: You have been compressing for 2 minutes. Switch with another rescuer if available.
cpr.switchDetail: Complete the switch in under 10 seconds to minimize interruptions.
cpr.aedInst: Turn on the AED and follow the voice prompts.
cpr.aedDetail: Attach pads to bare chest. Ensure no one is touching the patient during analysis.
cpr.strongSession: Strong session. Tighten the gaps below.
cpr.goodBase: Good base. Repeat this scenario once more.
cpr.reviewFundamentals: Review the CPR technique fundamentals before retrying.
cpr.cameraError: Camera did not start. Check the message on the left and try again.
cpr.upperBodyNote: Upper body visibility is enough for CPR feedback.
cpr.cameraSetupDesc: Hold still until your shoulders, elbows, and wrists are all visible. Training starts automatically once the view is locked in.
